nodes.name: KCKCC Internship
nodes.description:
Landed an internship that started part time through the school year 
then full time during summers.

Majority of my time was fixing bugs, updating content and the occational creative freedom
to add new pages and banners.

skills.name: HTML
skills.description: Learned to write semantic HTML in order to provide school's pages with searchable content.

skills.name: CSS
skills.description: Start learning CSS in school work and applying minor css styling to school content pages

skills.name: Macromedia Flash
skills.description: Used flash to build out animations and tweens for custom website ad banners

skills.name: Photoshop
skills.description: Built out ad banners for school site and produced page mockups for particular departments

skills.name: Perl
skills.description: Learned to work with Perl for content updates and form submissions

nodes.name: Virtumundo
nodes.description:
My first full time job outside of school!  Got hired on as a web designer where I got 
to spend some time engaging with Photoshop more.  Eventually got my hands on
some flash experience with marketing banners and forms.

skills.name: CSS
skills.description: Start using CSS to decorate landing pages and TreeLoot website.

skills.name: HTML
skills.description: Not only used HTML for general content updates, but started to learn to use HTML for building targeted advertising emails.

skills.name: Macromedia Flash
skills.description: Continued with animation and tweens then learned to apply ActionScripting to automate animations and add in logic for more interactive banners and games

nodes.name: MultiService
nodes.description:
Perfect entry level job where I got to help maintain the companies website but
had the opportunity to work on internal projects, tools and e-commerce stories.

Turned out this was the perfect incubator to kick-off a ton of exploration and 
experience with a lot of new tools and technology at the time.  Even got to spend some time
maintaining server racks for online games. 😅

skills.name: Macromedia Flash
skills.description: Built out animated banners and theme'd games to use for website promotions

skills.name: JavaScript
skills.description: I avoided JavasScript for a long time, but became inevitable the demand for interactions so started to embrace this tool more along with cross-browser frustrations.

skills.name: CSS
skills.description: I loved CSS Zen Garden at the time, so anything I could control with stylesheets while maintaining DOM integrity, was my obsession.

skills.name: Perl
skills.description: Spent time learning how to interface with MySQL and building out a custom CMS.  Also worked with Interchange ecommerce platform to help build out an electronics and stereo equipment store.

skills.name: MySQL
skills.description: Learned advanced indexing and querying capabilities to get related products on ecommerece stores.

nodes.name: DEG
nodes.description:
My first job dedicated to building content sites with CSS/HTML within a custom .NET framework.

DEG eventually started working with clients to help build projects in Wordpress, Magento and 
Sitecore/Sharepoint environments which expoded me to several other languages such as PHP/.NET/Node.

skills.name: CSS
skills.description: Spent a lot of time working with stylesheets and systemizing CSS on content sites with tools like SASS/LESS and applying through semantic HTML using atomic design principals.

skills.name: jQuery
skills.description: Used jQuery and wrote custom plugins to help build out splash page and content page interactions.

skills.name: Macromedia Flash
skills.description: JavaScript interactions were not as accessible as they are today, so setting up Flash components to add location map interactions and game-like interfaces were imperative to add unique interactions on client projects.

skills.name: .NET
skills.description: About 70% of projects with DEG were .NET related. I was hired initially to build out UI's for all of our custom .NET projects.

nodes.name: Crema
nodes.description:
Saught out Crema because I loved their values around building 
good products for users and customers.  This included 
working collaboratively in an agile environment allowing all roles to
contribute

Spent half my tenure here 
helping start-ups build out their product ideas and the 
other half helping enterprise organizations build out their 
internal tools.

skills.name: Wordpress
skills.description: Majority of clients' products needed support for marketing/CMS which I was able to take on building out custom themes and plugins.

skills.name: Angular
skills.description: Prior to adopting React, Angular was our primary tool to build out single page apps leveraging their unique templating system (at the time).

skills.name: React
skills.description: Entroduced to React through co-workers research and willingness to experiement with it's universal capabilities across web and native platforms.  Since then, have latched onto React due to it's community support and accessible learning curve.

skills.name: D3
skills.description: D3 with React is a great way to componentize visualizations using composable JSX components without D3's DOM utilities, leveraging and focusing on the core dataviz functions.

skills.name: Typescript
skills.description: Introduced to TS through a React/Redux project.  Definitely a humbling experience adding type assurance to an existing Javascript codebase, but the pay-off was worth it!
